source_jobs.title: Vice President of Marketing
source_jobs.description:
VICE PRESIDENT OF MARKETING



Please Note: This is an on-site position located in Atlanta, GA. Crisp is committed to supporting candidates by offering relocation assistance for qualified applicants who require moving to the Atlanta area.



About the Role

As the Vice President of Marketing , you will lead Crisp’s marketing department in a high-growth, sales-centered environment, unifying lead generation, paid media, AdOps, events, lifecycle/email, creative, brand, social, and podcast functions to drive revenue, retention, and client acquisition.

You will report to the Chief Commercial Officer and serve as the senior leader responsible for building a disciplined, high-urgency marketing engine that directly supports business growth. This role requires a process-oriented operator who can bring clarity to ambiguity, establish campaign schedules and accountability systems, improve email and campaign performance, and align marketing activity to measurable commercial outcomes.

The ideal candidate comes from a challenger brand, lifestyle brand, membership/coaching business, or prestige professional services environment where trust, differentiation, and a sophisticated sale were central to growth. You are a scrappy, resourceful builder with strong sales-centered marketing experience, deep ownership of lead generation, and the ability to elevate both performance marketing and creative execution. You must also be forward-thinking in your use of AI, with experience applying automation, emerging tools, or agentic AI workflows to improve marketing speed, quality, and efficiency.



Responsibilities:

 - Develop and own Crisp’s integrated marketing strategy across lead generation, paid media, AdOps, events, lifecycle/email, creative, brand, social, community and podcast.

 - Build a revenue-centered marketing engine that increases qualified leads, pipeline contribution, client acquisition, retention, and overall revenue growth.

 - Partner closely with Sales and Revenue leadership to improve lead quality, campaign performance, show rates, conversion, and sales enablement.

 - Lead paid media and AdOps strategy, ensuring campaigns are properly planned, tracked, optimized, reported, and tied to commercial outcomes.

 - Own lifecycle and email marketing strategy, including campaign calendars, segmentation, nurture programs, open rates, engagement, and conversion performance.

 - Drive event and membership-related marketing efforts that support acquisition, engagement, retention, and brand authority.

 - Elevate creative execution across video, design, campaign assets, social content, and brand storytelling, ensuring output is both high-quality and commercially effective.

 - Strengthen Crisp’s brand presence across social, podcast, thought leadership, content, and community-driven channels.

 - Implement clear marketing processes, operating rhythms, workflows, KPIs, dashboards, and accountability systems to improve speed, quality, and team performance.

 - Break down silos across marketing functions and build a culture of urgency, collaboration, ownership, and high standards.

 - Hire, coach, manage, and develop marketing leaders and specialists across creative, performance, brand, lifecycle, and operations.

 - Apply AI, automation, and emerging marketing technology to improve campaign execution, reporting, creative production, personalization, and team efficiency.

Requirements:

 - 10+ years of integrated marketing leadership experience, including ownership of multi-disciplinary teams across performance marketing, creative, brand, lifecycle/email, events, and marketing operations.

 - Strong sales-centered marketing experience with demonstrated impact on revenue, pipeline, lead generation, acquisition, and retention.

 - Proven success leading lead generation strategy in a sales-led or high-growth environment.

 - Hands-on understanding of paid media, AdOps, campaign tracking, attribution, optimization, and performance reporting.

 - Sig

source_jobs.title: Executive Coach
source_jobs.description:
EXECUTIVE COACH



Please Note: This role is on-site in Atlanta, GA. Monday - Friday for the first 90 days, with hybrid eligibility after 90 days (Tues/Thurs remote) except during designated blackout periods and key workshop weeks. Crisp supports relocation for qualified candidates who need to move to the Atlanta area



About the Role

If you thrive in a fast-paced environment, have a passion for helping business owners grow, and are excited to manage a large client caseload, you might be the perfect fit for our Coaching https://crisp.co/coaching/ team.

Our Executive Coaches develop and maintain close relationships with our largest clients to help them expand their status as market leaders. You will blend high-accountability coaching with practical business and operational guidance to drive measurable outcomes for clients.

As a member of our Coaching team, you will leverage a combination of consulting insights from both personal experience and Crisp’s playbook to provide masterful coaching that empowers clients to achieve results. You’ll be a guide, a strategist, a trusted advisor - the Yoda you’ve always been called to be.



Responsibilities:

 - Operate as the owner of your book of business, driving Crisp’s growth and stewarding each client relationship as a long-term revenue and impact investment.

 - Confidently lead clients through high-stakes, time-sensitive situations (e.g., executive or key employee departures, operational breakdowns, unclear KPIs, leadership misalignment), balancing immediate triage with long-term strategic alignment.

 - Demonstrated ability to deeply analyze P&Ls, identify margin and cost structure issues, and recommend concrete financial and operational changes. This is not just high-level insights.

 - This role is not a traditional, client led coaching position. Executive Coaches at Crisp are expected to utilize/blend accountability, consulting, and operational guidance. Candidates who prefer purely facilitative coaching without direct recommendations, metrics, or outcomes may not be a fit.

 - Operates with a “we over me” mindset, prioritizing what’s best for the client and Crisp over personal preference or convenience.

 - Build and maintain trusted relationships with business owners, helping them translate vision into reality by identifying growth opportunities, setting clear financial goals, and mapping actionable strategies.

 - Lead strategic coaching sessions that combine business insight, financial acumen, and operational guidance to drive tangible outcomes

 - Own and manage each client’s Strategic Growth Roadmap (“growth map”), adapting coaching focus to roadmap priorities and commitments made at workshops, focus retreats, and other Crisp events.

 - Document coaching calls and provide clear, concise summaries outlining action items, key takeaways, and resources for continued growth.

 - Identify operational, financial, and leadership blind spots and prescribe clear solutions to remove barriers to growth.

 - Drive client follow-through and utilization of key Crisp initiatives (e.g., recruiting support, shoots scheduled, workshop participation) through consistent accountability and structured follow-up.

 - Help clients integrate learnings from Crisp workshops and coaching content into their business practices - ensuring concepts are translated into action.

 - Hold clients accountable to what they say they want to achieve, balancing empathy and tough love to drive real progress.

 - Ensure clients fully engage with the Crisp Coach program - helping them maximize the return on their investment.

 - Travel: open to ~2x/quarter client visits as needed (not a fixed cadence).



Requirements:

 - 5–8+ years of proven business leadership experience with a track record of driving growth and building strong teams - ideally within operations, finance, sales, or client success functions in businesses generating $5M - $100M+ in annual revenue.

 - A strategic thinker who can spot opportunitie

source_jobs.title: Account Executive
source_jobs.description:
ACCOUNT EXECUTIVE



Please Note: This is an on-site position located in Atlanta, GA. Crisp is committed to supporting candidates by offering relocation assistance for qualified applicants who require moving to the Atlanta area.



About the Role

Top performers at Crisp will earn $250K–$350K without having to be a road warrior! We sell a proven service with a short sales cycle and without prospecting!! If you thrive in a fast-paced, high-performance environment and have a track record of closing deals, this is an opportunity to build a substantial income while advancing your sales career.

As part of Crisp's Account Executive team, you will be entrusted to lead meaningful conversations with the largest and most sophisticated law firm owners in the industry.

Crisp is looking for elite sales professionals who want to earn at a high level while selling a proven service through a streamlined sales process. This is not a cold-calling role. Our Account Executives work with qualified prospects and conduct consultations over Zoom using a proven two-call sales process.


What You’ll Do:

 - Conduct consultative sales meetings with qualified prospects via Zoom Manage opportunities through a structured two-call sales process

 - Build trust and credibility with prospective clients in the legal industry

 - Maintain accurate pipeline and follow-up activity

 - Consistently achieve and exceed revenue targets

 - Collaborate with sales leadership and team members to improve performance



What Makes This Role Different:

 - Healthy guaranteed base salary

 - Uncapped earning potential

 - Qualified prospect meetings provided

 - No extensive travel required

 - Entire sales process conducted over Zoom

 - Proven sales methodology

 - High-growth organization with significant advancement opportunities



Ideal Candidate:

 - You may be a strong fit if you have 5+ years of experience in B2B sales selling Legal services, Professional services, Business consulting, and/or Financial services.

 - Must be able to demonstrate history of consistent quota attainment

 - Experience selling consultatively to attorneys, entrepreneurs, and/or C-level executives.

 - Must have strong communication and presentation skills

 - Must be comfortable conducting sales meetings and closing deals over Zoom



Success Profile:

 - Highly competitive

 - Coachable

 - Process-oriented

 - Strong communicators

 - Results-driven

 - Motivated by significant earning potential



The Perks:

 - Full dental, vision, and medical coverage (100% employer-paid)

 - 4% retirement contribution match

 - Extensive paid time off policy

 - Paid leave for expanding families

 - Relocation assistance provided for out-of-state hires



Who We Are:

At Crisp, we are executing a mission to generate a $100B impact by empowering 10,000 law firms to scale their revenue by $10M each. We are North America’s premier law firm growth partner, backed by a nationally recognized brand and a proven history of excellence in legal marketing and executive coaching.

Our exponential growth—surpassing 1470% over three years—has secured our place on the Inc. 5000 for seven years running and established us as one of Atlanta’s fastest-growing organizations for nearly a decade.

We lead the legal sector with authoritative resources including the #1 best-selling book, The Game Changing Attorney; the industry’s top-ranked podcast; and the world’s largest law firm growth event, the Crisp Game Changers Summit.

For high-performers seeking unparalleled professional advancement, elite compensation, and the opportunity to drive meaningful change in the legal landscape, Crisp is the ultimate destination.



Curious whether Crisp is the right fit for you? We invite prospective candidates to book a 15-minute conversation https://calendly.com/lauren-hayhurst-crisp/new-meeting with a member of our Recruiting team to learn more about our Account Executive roles before applying.



#LI-AH2

source_jobs.title: Associate Sales Development Representative
source_jobs.description:
ASSOCIATE SALES DEVELOPMENT REPRESENTATIVE



Please Note: This is an on-site position located in Atlanta, GA. Crisp is committed to supporting candidates by offering relocation assistance for qualified applicants who require moving to the Atlanta area.



About the Role

As an Associate Sales Development Representative at Crisp, you are the force multiplier behind a Crisp Growth Advisor; the person ensuring they’re consistently in front of the right law firm owners, having the right conversations, at the right time.

This role reports to the Chief Commercial Officer while receiving direct day-to-day mentorship, coaching, and guidance from your paired Growth Advisor.

You own the engine behind the relationship-building process: outbound prospecting, CRM management, scheduling, pipeline organization, and converting inbound interest from our flagship podcast, conferences, events, referrals, and digital marketing into qualified discovery conversations.

The Growth Advisor leads the sales process and closes deals; your job is making sure the pipeline stays full and momentum never slows down.

This is a foundational role within Crisp’s Growth Team and a true career path, not a parking spot. We hire people who want their Growth Advisor to win today while preparing to become a Growth Advisor themselves in the future.



Why Be an Associate Sales Development Representative at Crisp?

 - Direct Mentorship & Career Growth:
   You’ll work directly alongside experienced closers, receiving real-time coaching on real sales conversations and learning the full sales cycle firsthand.

 - A Brand That Opens Doors:
   You won’t be cold-pitching an unknown company. Crisp https://crisp.co?utm_source=chatgpt.com is the #1 law firm growth company in America. Between the Crisp Game Changers Summit, the Game Changing Attorney Podcast, and our national reputation, there’s a strong chance prospects already know who we are.

 - A Proven Growth Engine:
   With 14+ years of refined sales and marketing systems, you’ll have strong messaging, high-quality inbound opportunities, and a clear path to success.

 - High-Level Conversations Daily:
   You’ll engage directly with law firm owners and managing partners through outbound outreach, events, referrals, podcasts, and inbound leads.

 - A Founding-Team Opportunity:
   This is a foundational role on a rapidly growing team where top performers can make a major impact and accelerate their careers quickly.



Responsibilities:

 - Run high-volume outbound outreach to law firm owners and managing partners across phone, email, LinkedIn, and event follow-up.

 - Qualify inbound leads from podcasts, conferences, referrals, and digital marketing campaigns.

 - Own CRM hygiene and pipeline management for your Growth Advisor’s pod inside HubSpot.

 - Coordinate scheduling, follow-up, and administrative support throughout the sales cycle.

 - Partner closely with marketing to maximize campaign and event conversion opportunities.

 - Represent Crisp at flagship events including the Crisp Game Changers Summit.

 - Consistently hit monthly targets for qualified meetings booked and qualified pipeline generated.



Requirements:

 - Credible, extroverted, and energetic communicator who can earn attention quickly.

 - Resilient and persistent — you don’t take “no” personally.

 - Highly coachable with a strong desire to grow into a closing role.

 - Proven ability to connect with people, stay disciplined, and pursue goals relentlessly.

 - High standards and strong ambition — good enough is never good enough.

 - Team-oriented mindset: you want your Growth Advisor to win while preparing for your own next step.

 - 0–3 years of full-time experience preferred; sales experience is a plus, not a requirement.



The Right Kind of Background:

The strongest candidates often come from relationship-driven, high-energy environments like:

 - Hospitality and events

 - Recruiting and talent acquisition

 - Membership or association sale

source_jobs.title: Director of Revenue Acceleration
source_jobs.description:
Director of Revenue Acceleration



Please Note: This is an on-site position located in Atlanta, GA. Crisp is committed to supporting candidates by offering relocation assistance for qualified applicants who require moving to the Atlanta area.

About the Role



Crisp is the #1 law firm growth company in America. We help committed law firm owners differentiate their firms, amplify their brands, and dominate their markets through transformational coaching, world-class video, and relentless digital marketing.

Crisp is currently at $55M in revenue and targeting $100M. The primary constraint on that growth is not the product or the sales process. It is commercial infrastructure. The lead-to-cash function today operates across fragmented tools, inconsistent data, and manual workflows.

As Director of Revenue Acceleration , you will be responsible for changing that. This is a Director-level position reporting directly to the Chief Commercial Officer. The successful candidate will inherit a small team and take end-to-end ownership of revenue operations, from first marketing touch through collected payment. The scope includes technology selection and implementation, process design, performance reporting, and AI-powered sales intelligence. This is an operational leadership role, not an advisory one.

The right person for this role has been involved in Lead 2 Cash and is excited by the chance to build a best-in-class version here. You want to own it and build it, you want to make the decisions and own the execution. You have an outcome focused mindset and every decision you make is in support of increasing efficiency or effectiveness for our sales team. You are open to more risk/reward in your compensation than might be typical for this kind of role.



What You’ll Own:



1. Full Funnel Ownership

This role carries accountability for performance metrics at every stage of the revenue funnel, from initial paid impression through collected payment.



2. Client Journey Architecture

Crisp's sales process is structured around three key virtual touch points. A significant part of this role involves ensuring that the journey between those touch points is deliberate, connected, and commercially purposeful. Prospective clients should arrive at each meeting already familiar with Crisp's work, already persuaded by the evidence, and ready to engage at a deeper level. That outcome does not happen by accident.

This function is responsible for designing and operating the full client journey infrastructure, from initial market warm-up through to signed agreement. Content creation sits with the marketing team. Process design, distribution, sequencing, and performance measurement sit here.

3. Onboarding & Sales Education

Get our reps up to speed quickly and continue their education throughout their time with us.



4. AI-Powered Sales Intelligence

Crisp is investing in AI-powered sales intelligence as a core part of its performance management infrastructure. This role leads that effort.



5. Team Leadership

This role inherits a small Revenue Operations team on day one. The Director is responsible for the output and development of that team, and for building the function's capacity as the business scales.



6. Reporting and Executive Visibility

This role owns the weekly revenue dashboard reviewed by the CCO and the broader leadership team. Data accuracy and actionable insight are both non-negotiable. When gaps or problems are identified, the expectation is that they are surfaced promptly and resolved.



What We’re Looking For:



The ideal candidate is highly metric-driven and process-oriented, with demonstrated experience building revenue operations infrastructure for a services or professional services business. Strong candidates will bring the following:

 - Detail orientation: A disciplined approach to data accuracy, with a strong instinct for identifying gaps or inconsistencies in reporting before they affect decisions.

 - Full funnel fl

source_jobs.title: Director of Revenue Optimization

This function is responsible for designing and operating the full client journey infrastructure, from initial market warm-up through to signed agreement. Content creation sits with the marketing team. Process design, distribution, sequencing, and performance measurement sit here.

3. Onboarding & Sales Education

Get our reps up to speed quickly and continue their education throughout their time with us.



4. AI-Powered Sales Intelligence

Crisp is investing in AI-powered sales intelligence as a core part of its performance management infrastructure. This role leads that effort.



5. Team Leadership

This role inherits a small Revenue Operations team on day one. The Director is responsible for the output and development of that team, and for building the function's capacity as the business scales.



6. Reporting and Executive Visibility

This role owns the weekly revenue dashboard reviewed by the CCO and the broader leadership team. Data accuracy and actionable insight are both non-negotiable. When gaps or problems are identified, the expectation is that they are surfaced promptly and resolved.



What We’re Looking For:



The ideal candidate is highly metric-driven and process-oriented, with demonstrated experience building revenue operations infrastructure for a services or professional services business. Strong candidates will bring the following:

 - Detail orientation: A disciplined approach to data accuracy, with a strong instinct for identifying gaps or inconsistencies in reporting before they affect decisions.

 - Full funnel fl

source_jobs.title: Sales Development Representative
source_jobs.description:
SALES DEVELOPMENT REPRESENTATIVE



Please Note: This is an on-site position located in Atlanta, GA. Crisp is committed to supporting candidates by offering relocation assistance for qualified applicants who require moving to the Atlanta area.



About the Role

As a Sales Development Representative at Crisp, you are the force multiplier behind a Crisp Growth Advisor; the person ensuring they’re consistently in front of the right law firm owners, having the right conversations, at the right time.

This role reports to the Chief Commercial Officer while receiving direct day-to-day mentorship, coaching, and guidance from your paired Growth Advisor.

You own the engine behind the relationship-building process: outbound prospecting, CRM management, scheduling, pipeline organization, and converting inbound interest from our flagship podcast, conferences, events, referrals, and digital marketing into qualified discovery conversations.

The Growth Advisor leads the sales process and closes deals; your job is making sure the pipeline stays full and momentum never slows down.

This is a foundational role within Crisp’s Growth Team and a true career path, not a parking spot. We hire people who want their Growth Advisor to win today while preparing to become a Growth Advisor themselves in the future.



Why Be a Sales Development Representative at Crisp?

 - Direct Mentorship & Career Growth:
   You’ll work directly alongside experienced closers, receiving real-time coaching on real sales conversations and learning the full sales cycle firsthand.

 - A Brand That Opens Doors:
   You won’t be cold-pitching an unknown company. Crisp https://crisp.co?utm_source=chatgpt.com is the #1 law firm growth company in America. Between the Crisp Game Changers Summit, the Game Changing Attorney Podcast, and our national reputation, there’s a strong chance prospects already know who we are.

 - A Proven Growth Engine:
   With 14+ years of refined sales and marketing systems, you’ll have strong messaging, high-quality inbound opportunities, and a clear path to success.

 - High-Level Conversations Daily:
   You’ll engage directly with law firm owners and managing partners through outbound outreach, events, referrals, podcasts, and inbound leads.

 - A Founding-Team Opportunity:
   This is a foundational role on a rapidly growing team where top performers can make a major impact and accelerate their careers quickly.



Responsibilities:

 - Run high-volume outbound outreach to law firm owners and managing partners across phone, email, LinkedIn, and event follow-up.

 - Qualify inbound leads from podcasts, conferences, referrals, and digital marketing campaigns.

 - Own CRM hygiene and pipeline management for your Growth Advisor’s pod inside HubSpot.

 - Coordinate scheduling, follow-up, and administrative support throughout the sales cycle.

 - Partner closely with marketing to maximize campaign and event conversion opportunities.

 - Represent Crisp at flagship events including the Crisp Game Changers Summit.

 - Consistently hit monthly targets for qualified meetings booked and qualified pipeline generated.



Requirements:

 - Credible, extroverted, and energetic communicator who can earn attention quickly.

 - Resilient and persistent — you don’t take “no” personally.

 - Highly coachable with a strong desire to grow into a closing role.

 - Proven ability to connect with people, stay disciplined, and pursue goals relentlessly.

 - High standards and strong ambition — good enough is never good enough.

 - Team-oriented mindset: you want your Growth Advisor to win while preparing for your own next step.

 - 0–3 years of full-time experience preferred; sales experience is a plus, not a requirement.



The Right Kind of Background:

The strongest candidates often come from relationship-driven, high-energy environments like:

 - Hospitality and events

 - Recruiting and talent acquisition

 - Membership or association sales

 - Fundraising and donor rela

notes: High-growth sales-centered environment with multiple sales and marketing roles
